title: Sliding Window Maximum
description: Given an integer array nums and an integer k, return the maximum sliding window for each sliding window of size k. You must solve the problem in O(n) time complexity.

examples.explanation: For the first window [1,3,-1], the maximum is 3. For the second window [3,-1,-3], the maximum is still 3. This continues through the array.
Each element is its own window. The maximum for the first window [1] is 1 and for [-1] is -1.
The maximums for sliding windows are calculated as follows: [9,10,9,-7,-4] gives 10, [10,9,-7,-4,-8] gives 10, etc.

hints: Consider using a deque to maintain the indices of the maximum elements.
You only need to keep indices of elements that are within the current window.
Make sure to remove indices that are out of the current window's range.
Think about how to efficiently update the maximum when the window slides.
